title: Chicago · Forty Years of Crime
subtitle: FBI UCR 1986–2002 + Chicago PD incidents 2003–2026 · 77 community areas · real locations

copy.chapter2Title: The map comes alive — 77 community areas
copy.chapter2Caption: Live monthly reports by community area. Every dot is a real reported incident location (block-level, sampled).
copy.transitionDesc: From here every record is incident-level across 77 community areas — real monthly counts, and every dot a real reported location, anonymized to the block.
copy.methodRecentSub: 2003–2026 · by community area, month by month · real incident locations
copy.methodDotsSub: Every dot is a real reported incident location (block-level). 88.6% of records map to a community area — the rest counted, disclosed, never invented.
copy.methodFootnote: FBI UCR (through 2002) and CPD incident data (2003+) are different measures — shown as two chapters, not one line.
copy.quizQuestion: Which community area is Chicago's safest?
copy.sourceLine: Data: Chicago Police Department via data.cityofchicago.org · block-level locations · modified from its original source
copy.coverageText: 88.6% of records mapped to a community area · pre-2003 counted citywide
copy.creditsSources: Sources: Chicago Police Department (CLEAR) incident data via data.cityofchicago.org (2001–present) & FBI UCR (1986–2002) via the FBI Crime Data Explorer. Community area boundaries: City of Chicago. Data modified for use from its original source, www.cityofchicago.org, the official website of the City of Chicago; the City makes no claims as to content, accuracy, timeliness, or completeness.

historyNotes.text: 1991: the peak — 320,332 crimes reported in one year, 86,945 of them violent.
The early-'90s peak holds through mid-decade — then the long decline begins.
By 2002, reports fall to 192,221 — down 40% from the 1991 peak.

annotations.text: August 2003 — the busiest month in the modern record: 44,271 reports citywide.
Every dot is a real reported incident location — anonymized to the block by the City of Chicago.
Austin — Chicago's busiest community area for Group A crime. Even here, reports fell 62% between 2003 and 2025.
The Loop fell only 10% since 2003 — the smallest decline of any community area.
Citywide, reports halved: 475,949 in 2003 → 237,497 in 2025. Every one of the 77 community areas fell.
